statements: SELECT FIRST sale FOR sale IN orderlines END AS first_sale FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY first_sale
SELECT FIRST sale FOR sale IN orderlines WHEN sale.qty > 1 AND sale.productId = "coffee01" END AS dos FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY dos

SELECT ANY thing IN orderlines[1].thing satisfies qty = 1 END AS things, array_length(orderlines), FIRST sale FOR sale IN orderlines END AS first_sale FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY id
SELECT id FROM orders WHERE FIRST sale FOR sale IN orderlines WHEN length(orderlines[0].productId) > 6 END AND test_id="order_func" ORDER BY id
SELECT FIRST sale FOR sale IN orderlines END AS A FROM orders WHERE poly_length(orderlines[0].productId) > 6 AND test_id="order_func" ORDER BY A
SELECT id, FIRST sale FOR sale IN orderlines END AS first_sale FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY first_sale, id
SELECT custId, FIRST sale FOR sale IN orderlines WHEN orderlines[0].productId > custId END as chosen_one FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY custId
SELECT ANY thing IN orderlines[0].thing satisfies qty = 1 END AS things, FIRST sale FOR sale IN orderlines END as orderlin FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY things, orderlin
SELECT id, (orderlines[0].qty + orderlines[1].qty) AS totquant FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY totquant, id
SELECT id, (orderlines[0].qty + orderlines[1].qty) FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY totquant, id
SELECT id, (orderlines[0].qty + orderlines[1].qty) AS totquant FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY totquant DESC, id ASC
SELECT id, orderlines[0].*, (orderlines[0].qty + orderlines[1].qty) AS totquant FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY totquant, id DESC
SELECT orderlines[1] FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY orderlines[1].qty, orderlines[1].productId
SELECT orderlines[0] FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY orderlines[0].productId, orderlines[0].qty ASC LIMIT 1
SELECT orderlines[0] FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY orderlines[0].productId, orderlines[0].qty ASC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 2
SELECT id, custId, orderlines[0].productId AS pId FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY pId, custId
SELECT custId, COUNT(1) AS cnt FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" GROUP BY custId ORDER BY custId, test_id
error: Expression (`orders`.`test_id`) (near line 1, column 104) must depend only on group keys or aggregates.

SELECT orderlines[0] FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY orderlines[0].productId, orderlines[0].qty ASC LIMIT 0 OFFSET 0
SELECT orderlines[0] FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY orderlines[0].productId, orderlines[0].qty ASC LIMIT -1 OFFSET 0
SELECT orderlines[0] FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY orderlines[0].productId, orderlines[0].qty DESC LIMIT -1

SELECT orderlines[0] FROM orders WHERE test_id="order_func" ORDER BY orderlines[0].productId, orderlines[0].qty ASC OFFSET 8192 LIMIT 1

description: order_limit_prune_sort: Tests full sort with no pruning
statements: SELECT custId, `shipped-on` FROM orders USE INDEX (order_cust) WHERE custId is valued AND test_id="order_limit_prune_sort" ORDER BY custId, `shipped-on`

explain.statement: SELECT true AS present FROM $explan AS p WHERE ANY v WITHIN p.plan.`~children` SATISFIES v.`#operator` = 'Order' AND v.partial_sort_term_count = 1 END

statements: SELECT custId, `shipped-on` FROM orders USE INDEX (order_cust) WHERE custId is valued AND test_id="order_limit_prune_sort" ORDER BY custId,`shipped-on` limit 4

description: order_limit_prune_sort: Tests offset + limit still using reduced sort count
statements: SELECT custId, `shipped-on` FROM orders USE INDEX (order_cust) WHERE custId is valued AND test_id="order_limit_prune_sort" ORDER BY custId, `shipped-on` offset 4 limit 2

description: order_limit_prune_sort: Tests offset + limit using reduced sort count produces correct results
statements: SELECT custId, `shipped-on` FROM orders USE INDEX (order_cust) WHERE custId is valued AND test_id="order_limit_prune_sort" ORDER BY custId, `shipped-on` offset 5 limit 2

description: order_limit_prune_sort: Tests offset + limit using reduced sort count with multiple partial sort keys
statements: SELECT custId, `shipped-on` FROM orders USE INDEX (order_cust_shipped) WHERE custId is valued AND test_id="order_limit_prune_sort" ORDER BY custId, `shipped-on` DESC, orderId offset 4 limit 2

explain.statement: SELECT true AS present FROM $explan AS p WHERE ANY v WITHIN p.plan.`~children` SATISFIES v.`#operator` = 'Order' AND v.partial_sort_term_count = 1 END AND ANY y WITHIN p.plan.`~children` SATISFIES y.`#operator` LIKE 'IndexScan%' AND y.covers IS NOT MISSING END

statements: SELECT custId, `shipped-on`, orderId FROM orders WHERE custId is valued AND test_id='order_limit_prune_sort' ORDER BY custId, `shipped-on` DESC, orderId offset 5 limit 2

description: order_limit_prune_sort: Tests offset + limit using reduced sort count and covering index scan
statements: SELECT custId, `shipped-on`, orderId FROM orders WHERE custId is valued AND test_id='order_limit_prune_sort' ORDER BY custId, `shipped-on` DESC, orderId offset 5 limit 2
